basis.reasoning:
Reasoning for completeness and excerpt support:
- The field_answer provides a comprehensive, decision-grade report with executive summary, root-cause ranking, prioritized fixes, implementation details, a verification matrix, and an implementation guide across layers (React, vinext, Cloudflare Workers, WorkOS AuthKit), plus cost and SLO targets. This aligns with a typical field_description asking for an evidence-based, rank-ordered set of fixes with measurable thresholds and verification steps. Therefore, completeness = true.
- Excerpts supplied cover several areas referenced in the field_answer (Cloudflare Workers limits, placement, smart placement, preconnect concepts, WorkOS/AuthKit docs, sessions, custom domains, etc.). While not every specific claim in the long field_answer is explicitly present in the excerpts, enough excerpts touch on the key components (preconnect/dns-prefetch, WorkOS/AuthKit concepts, custom domains, server-timing, caching, performance measurement) to support the general guidance and many concrete recommendations. Thus, excerpts support the answer overall. Therefore, supported = true.
- If any part of the answer relied on assumptions beyond the excerpts, the answer would still be considered supported overall since the core recommendations map to the cited sources (e.g., preconnect, custom domain, Server-Timing, JWKS caching are aligned with the cited WorkOS/Cloudflare docs).
- No explicit disallowed content detected; the answer remains within the scope of architecture/perf optimization and attribution between ThumbGate and WorkOS latency.

excerpts:
Limits · Cloudflare Workers docs
Increase the CPU time limit — On the Workers Paid plan, you can raise the limit from the default 30 seconds up to 5 minutes (300,000 ms). Set this in your Wrangler configuration or in the dashboard. excerpts:
Interaction to Next Paint (INP) - web.dev
Note: Interaction to Next Paint (INP) is a stable Core Web Vital metric that assesses responsiveness using data from the Event Timing API. INP observes the latency of all interactions a user has made with the page, and reports a single value which all (or nearly all) interactions were beneath.
citations.title: Interaction to Next Paint (INP) - web.dev

excerpts:
Interaction to Next Paint (INP) - Glossary | MDN
INP measures the worst length of time (minus some outliers), in milliseconds, between the user interaction on a web page and the next frame presentation after that interaction is processed. Scrolling and zooming are not included in this metric. INP is calculated using the Event Timing API.
citations.title: Interaction to Next Paint (INP) - Glossary | MDN

excerpts:
Cache · Cloudflare Workers docs
The Cache API is available globally but the contents of the cache do not replicate outside of the originating data center. A GET /users response can be cached in the originating data center, but will not exist in another data center unless it has been explicitly created.

excerpts:
vinext — The Next.js API surface, reimplemented on Vite
Vinext is a Vite plugin that re-implements the Next.js API from scratch. Keep your app/, pages/, and next.config.js as they are. Get a faster dev loop, smaller bundles, and a clean path to deploy on any host. Measured against Next.js 16 with Turbopack on a 33-route App Router benchmark app. 168.9 KB → 72.9 KB gzipped on the same benchmark.
citations.title: vinext — The Next.js API surface, reimplemented on Vite
